text: A structured 6-week novice running program significantly increases the percentage of participants meeting health-enhancing physical activity guidelines, with effects sustained at 6 months.
Novice runners completing a structured start-to-run program show significantly greater compliance with vigorous-intensity physical activity norms compared to matched controls at 6 months.
The physical activity gains from a structured novice running program are primarily driven by increases in vigorous-intensity activities and sports activities rather than light or moderate activity.
Organized sports club settings are effective channels for delivering beginner running programs that produce sustained increases in physical activity among previously inactive adults.
Without structured intervention, sedentary adults matched for age and sex show no significant changes in physical activity levels over a 6-month period.
